label: 3-month colectomy rate with upfront intensified infliximab (10 mg/kg) in acute severe UC, versus an estimated 27% standard-dose reference
source: Goyal MK et al. Inflamm Bowel Dis. 2026 Apr 24 (PMID: 42030468) — Bayesian meta-analysis incorporating PREDICT-UC and historical standard-dose infliximab trials in acute severe ulcerative colitis

label: clinical remission at weeks 52-56 with ustekinumab in a multicenter real-world UC cohort
source: Baek JE et al. Intestinal Research. 2026 Apr 20 (PMID: 42002972) — 60 Korean UC patients; remission was 49.2% at week 8, 59.3% at weeks 16-20, and treatment persistence reached 84.9% at 12 months

label: 12-month ustekinumab treatment persistence in real-world UC
source: Baek JE et al. Intestinal Research. 2026 Apr 20 (PMID: 42002972) — persistence remained high despite prior advanced therapy exposure in part of the cohort

label: clinical remission at week 12 with fully subcutaneous guselkumab induction vs placebo in moderate-to-severe UC
source: Peyrin-Biroulet L et al. Lancet Gastroenterol Hepatol. 2026 (PMID: 41544637) — phase 3 ASTRO trial; week-24 clinical remission reached 35-36% with guselkumab vs 9% with placebo, with no new safety concerns

label: colectomy within 1 year after severe UC hospitalization in patients already exposed to advanced therapy
source: Raine T et al. J Crohns Colitis. 2026 Apr 8 (PMID: 41978350) — real-world US cohort of 9,716 adults hospitalized for severe UC; overall 12.2% colectomy during admission and 20.4% colectomy within 1 year

label: UC-related rehospitalization within 1 year after discharge without colectomy
source: Raine T et al. J Crohns Colitis. 2026 Apr 8 (PMID: 41978350) — nested discharge cohort after severe UC hospitalization

label: higher odds of week-52 clinical remission with second-line vedolizumab after failure of one anti-TNF
source: Lukas M et al. Ther Adv Gastroenterol. 2026 (PMID: 41883697) — patient-level meta-analysis of GEMINI-1 and VISIBLE-1; also improved steroid-free remission, Mayo endoscopic score ≤1, and health-related quality of life

label: relapse risk after stopping rectal 5-ASA monotherapy in quiescent UC
source: Arzivian A et al. Inflamm Bowel Dis. 2026 Apr 1 (PMID: 41338243) — systematic review and meta-analysis of 29 studies

label: relapse risk after stopping oral 5-ASA monotherapy in quiescent UC
source: Arzivian A et al. Inflamm Bowel Dis. 2026 Apr 1 (PMID: 41338243) — withdrawal signal confined to monotherapy, not clearly seen after escalation to biologics or immunomodulators

label: maintenance clinical response with oral S1P receptor modulators vs placebo in moderate-to-severe UC
source: Gardezi SAA et al. Dig Dis Sci. 2026 Apr 13 (PMID: 41968221) — meta-analysis of 8 RCTs, 2,663 patients across etrasimod, ozanimod, tamuzimod, and KRP203

label: maintenance histological remission with oral S1P receptor modulators vs placebo
source: Gardezi SAA et al. Dig Dis Sci. 2026 Apr 13 (PMID: 41968221) — tissue-level healing signal across the S1P class

label: clinical remission with FMT when delivered by colonoscopy + enema vs control
source: Huang B et al. Adv Ther. 2026 Apr 7 (PMID: 41945234) — updated meta-analysis of 16 RCTs; strongest route-specific effect in UC

label: clinical remission rate — tulisokibart (anti-TL1A) vs placebo (26% vs 1%) in moderate-to-severe IBD, Phase 2 ARTEMIS-UC. Phase 3 now enrolling
source: Goyal MK et al. Aliment Pharmacol Ther. 2026 Apr 6 (PMID: 41941212) — review of TL1A/DR3 clinical evidence; tulisokibart Phase 2 data

label: histo-endoscopic mucosal healing at induction with IL-23 inhibitors vs placebo (7 RCTs, 4,203 patients)
source: Amin HM et al. Int J Colorectal Dis. 2025 (PMID: 41467943) — mirikizumab, risankizumab, guselkumab

label: serious adverse events with IL-23 inhibitors vs placebo during induction (RR 0.39) — active UC control reduces disease-driven harms
source: Amin HM et al. Int J Colorectal Dis. 2025 (PMID: 41467943) — 7 RCTs, 4,203 patients

label: mucosal healing at 40 weeks with etrasimod vs placebo (I²=0% — zero heterogeneity)
source: GRADE meta-analysis, 4 RCTs, 1239 patients — Jha A et al. Ann Pharmacother. 2026 (PMID: 41891290)

label: mirikizumab clinical remission sustained at 4 years (W52 remitters)
source: LUCENT-3 OLE — Sands BE et al. Inflamm Bowel Dis. 2026 (PMID: 41777218)
